# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Summary

Migrate 5 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. Since MSTest 3.8.0 ≥ 3.2.0, no MSTest version upgrade is needed. Since .NET 8 SDK < .NET 10, the `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` bridge property is required and MTP arguments must be passed after `--`.

---

## Phase 1: Prepare Directory.Build.props

Add/update `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root with:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> **Why the condition?** Use `MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests')` (adjust to your naming convention) — `IsTestProject` is not available when `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated.

> **Why `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`?** On .NET 8/9 SDK, this enables `dotnet test` to discover and run MTP-based test projects.

---

## Phase 2: Install MTP Extension Packages

Add these NuGet packages to each test project (or via `Directory.Build.props` / `Directory.Packages.props`):

| Package | Purpose | Replaces |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` (1.6.2) | TRX report generation | `--logger trx` |
| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage` (17.13.0) | Code coverage collection | `--collect "Code Coverage"` |

```xml
<ItemGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
</ItemGroup>
```

---

## Phase 3: Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

### Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2

```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --no-build --configuration Release
      -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

### Publish Test Results (TRX)

```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
  condition: always()
```

### Publish Code Coverage

```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
  condition: always()
```

---

## Phase 4: Handle Behavioral Differences

### Exit code 8 (zero tests discovered)

MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are found (VSTest returned 0). If conditional test filtering may yield zero tests, add to `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### Argument separator

On .NET 8 SDK, MTP-specific arguments go after `--`:

```shell
dotnet test --no-build -- --report-trx --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MyClass"
```

---

## Phase 5: Remove VSTest-Only Packages (after verification)

Once migration is confirmed working, remove from each test project:

- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — not needed for MTP
- `MSTest.TestAdapter` — not needed; `EnableMSTestRunner` replaces adapter-based discovery

Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` (or just `MSTest` meta-package if used).

---

## Phase 6: Verify

1. `dotnet build` — zero errors
2. `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match pre-migration baseline
3. Run test executable directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe --list-tests`
4. CI pipeline produces TRX artifacts and code coverage reports
5. Visual Studio Test Explorer discovers and runs tests

---

## CLI Argument Translation Reference

| VSTest | MTP | Notes |
|--------|-----|-------|
| `--filter <expr>` | `-- --filter <expr>` | Same syntax for MSTest |
| `--logger trx` | `-- --report-trx` | Needs TrxReport package |
| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `-- --coverage` | Needs CodeCoverage package |
| `--blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `-- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` | Needs HangDump package |
| `--blame-crash` | `-- --crashdump` | Needs CrashDump package |
| `--results-directory <dir>` | `-- --results-directory <dir>` | Same |
| `--settings <file>` | `-- --settings <file>` | Same |

---

## Common Pitfalls

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Mixing VSTest + MTP projects | Migrate all 5 projects together |
| Forgetting `--` separator | Required on .NET 8 SDK — MTP args must come after `--` |
| Exit code 8 breaks CI | Use `--ignore-exit-code 8` if zero-test scenarios are expected |
| Missing `OutputType Exe` | Required for MTP; set in Directory.Build.props |
| `IsTestProject` condition | Don't use it in Directory.Build.props — use name-based condition |

---

## References

- [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
- [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
- [MTP extensions](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-platform-extensions)

+
+---
+
+## Phase 2: Install MTP Extension Packages
+
+Add these NuGet packages to each test project (or via `Directory.Build.props` / `Directory.Packages.props`):
+
+| Package | Purpose | Replaces |
+|---------|---------|----------|
+| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` (1.6.2) | TRX report generation | `--logger trx` |
+| `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage` (17.13.0) | Code coverage collection | `--collect "Code Coverage"` |
+
+```xml
+<ItemGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
+  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
+  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
+</ItemGroup>
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 3: Update Azure DevOps Pipeline
+
+### Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2
+
+```yaml
+# BEFORE
+- task: VSTest@3
+  inputs:
+    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
+    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
+    codeCoverageEnabled: true
+
+# AFTER
+- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
+  displayName: 'Run Tests'
+  inputs:
+    command: 'test'
+    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
+    arguments: >-
+      --no-build --configuration Release
+      -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
+      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
+```
+
+### Publish Test Results (TRX)
+
+```yaml
+- task: PublishTestResults@2
+  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
+  inputs:
+    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
+    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
+  condition: always()
+```
+
+### Publish Code Coverage
+
+```yaml
+- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
+  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
+  inputs:
+    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
+  condition: always()
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 4: Handle Behavioral Differences
+
+### Exit code 8 (zero tests discovered)
+
+MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are found (VSTest returned 0). If conditional test filtering may yield zero tests, add to `Directory.Build.props`:
+
+```xml
+<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
+  <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
+</PropertyGroup>
+```
+
+### Argument separator
+
+On .NET 8 SDK, MTP-specific arguments go after `--`:
+
+```shell
+dotnet test --no-build -- --report-trx --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MyClass"
+```
+
+---
+
+## Phase 5: Remove VSTest-Only Packages (after verification)
+
+Once migration is confirmed working, remove from each test project:
+
+- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — not needed for MTP
+- `MSTest.TestAdapter` — not needed; `EnableMSTestRunner` replaces adapter-based discovery
+
+Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` (or just `MSTest` meta-package if used).
+
+---
+
+## Phase 6: Verify
+
+1. `dotnet build` — zero errors
+2. `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match pre-migration baseline
+3. Run test executable directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe --list-tests`
+4. CI pipeline produces TRX artifacts and code coverage reports
+5. Visual Studio Test Explorer discovers and runs tests
+
+---
+
+## CLI Argument Translation Reference
+
+| VSTest | MTP | Notes |
+|--------|-----|-------|
+| `--filter <expr>` | `-- --filter <expr>` | Same syntax for MSTest |
+| `--logger trx` | `-- --report-trx` | Needs TrxReport package |
+| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `-- --coverage` | Needs CodeCoverage package |
+| `--blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `-- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` | Needs HangDump package |
+| `--blame-crash` | `-- --crashdump` | Needs CrashDump package |
+| `--results-directory <dir>` | `-- --results-directory <dir>` | Same |
+| `--settings <file>` | `-- --settings <file>` | Same |
+
+---
+
+## Common Pitfalls
+
+| Risk | Mitigation |
+|------|------------|
+| Mixing VSTest + MTP projects | Migrate all 5 projects together |
+| Forgetting `--` separator | Required on .NET 8 SDK — MTP args must come after `--` |
+| Exit code 8 breaks CI | Use `--ignore-exit-code 8` if zero-test scenarios are expected |
+| Missing `OutputType Exe` | Required for MTP; set in Directory.Build.props |
+| `IsTestProject` condition | Don't use it in Directory.Build.props — use name-based condition |
+
+---
+
+## References
+
+- [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
+- [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
+- [MTP extensions](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-platform-extensions)
+
